concept.shortdesc: The Operation Section is a reference for the new operator and a refresher for the

conbody.p: The Operation Section is a reference for the new operator and a refresher for the experienced one. This section includes a discussion of gauges, switches, lift truck controls, attachment controls, transportation and towing information.
Photographs and illustrations guide the operator through correct procedures of checking, starting, operating and stopping the lift truck.
Operating techniques outlined in this publication are basic. Skill and techniques develop as the operator gains knowledge of the lift truck and its capabilities.
Before operating, inspect the lift truck to ensure it is in safe working condition. Check fluid levels, tyre pressure, and all safety devices.
Always fasten the seatbelt before operating the lift truck. Ensure the overhead guard is in place and undamaged.
Check all controls and instruments for proper operation before beginning work. Report any defects immediately to your supervisor.
Page 138 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
